answer:
We have  
\[
\log_x(y^x) = 10 \quad\Rightarrow\quad x \log_x y = 10 \quad\Rightarrow\quad \log_x y = \frac{10}{x}.
\]  
Similarly,  
\[
\log_y(x^{4y}) = 10 \quad\Rightarrow\quad 4y \log_y x = 10 \quad\Rightarrow\quad \log_y x = \frac{10}{4y} = \frac{5}{2y}.
\]  
Since \(\log_x y \cdot \log_y x = 1\), we get  
\[
\frac{10}{x} \cdot \frac{5}{2y} = 1 \quad\Rightarrow\quad \frac{50}{2xy} = 1 \quad\Rightarrow\quad \frac{25}{xy} = 1 \quad\Rightarrow\quad xy = 25.
\]

\[
\boxed{25}
\]
